Issue: ImageStats.__call__ crashes with UnboundLocalError when 'nda_croppeds' is pre-populated in the input dict
Decision: Added the missing else branch to read the pre-computed 'nda_croppeds' value from the dict and wrapped the method body in try/finally to guarantee torch.set_grad_enabled is always restored
Rationale: The fix ensures that when 'nda_croppeds' is provided in the input dict, it is read correctly instead of causing an UnboundLocalError. The try/finally block ensures proper restoration of torch.set_grad_enabled state, addressing a secondary issue.
Author: williams145
Risk: Potential silent failures if pre-computed 'nda_croppeds' has wrong type or mismatched channel count (addressed by explicit validation raising ValueError)

Argument.author: williams145
Argument.argument: PR #8803 by @bluehyena covers overlapping ground on issue #8802, but this PR adds explicit validation of the pre-computed nda_croppeds value, raising a clear ValueError for wrong type or mismatched channel count rather than propagating silently to a confusing downstream error

Argument.author: williams145
Argument.argument: This PR also fixes an unrelated issue: the UnboundLocalError when nda_croppeds is pre-computed in the input dict, which is independent of the grad state discussion
